prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack) and the dual descriptor for gas-phase phenol; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic index f⁺.
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ (= B3LYP/def2-TZVP, RI density fitting) in PySCF.
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied fixed geometry (file “/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz”). No re-optimization was performed – this is a single-point Fukui analysis at B3LYP/def2-TZVP on that structure (DFT//input-XYZ).
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge / multiplicity: neutral singlet for the N-electron reference (charge 0, mult 1); cation state (N–1) run as charge +1, doublet; anion state (N+1) run as charge –1, doublet.
• Software: chemkit “fukui-reactivity” skill (backend PySCF 2.x; Mulliken charges).
• Convergence & caveats: integrity.status = “ok”, integrity.trustworthy = true. SCF cycles converged for all three charge–multiplicity states. Engine issued three warnings (verbatim below) noting the check that Σf⁺ = Σf⁻ = 1.0000 and presence of the common schema header. No other warnings or fall-backs reported.

Key numerical result
• Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, 0-based index 2) with f⁺ = 2.1028319
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, 0-based index 0) with f⁻ = 0.16201477
• Arrays of all atom-condensed indices (13 atoms):
